input.thought: Open official Podio pages for API, authentication, pricing, and rate limits to verify structured fields from first-party sources.

arguments.task: Research Podio using only official first-party pages and return a compact evidence-backed summary for these fields: one-line product description, documented API authentication methods, API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None) and breadth, rate limit notes, whether Podio offers an official MCP server or MCP docs, access tier for API use (self-serve free, self-serve trial, paid plan required, admin approval required, or partner gated/contact sales) with explicit evidence from pricing or developer pages, and buildability for an AI-agent toolkit. Requirements: open and read live canonical pages, not search snippets; prefer developers.podio.com, podio.com/pricing, and any official support/help pages. Include the exact URLs visited that support each conclusion. If a page is JS-rendered or inaccessible, note that clearly and try another official page. Do not use third-party sources.

raw_result.description: No-code work management platform for building customizable team workspaces, workflows, and project processes.
raw_result.auth_notes: Podio documents OAuth 2.0 authentication plus an app authentication flow; the auth page lists server-side, client-side, username/password, and app authentication flows using client ID and client secret.
raw_result.gating_reason: Podio's official pricing page states the Free plan includes API access with 1,000 API calls per hour, with paid add-ons for higher volume.

api_surface.breadth: Podio provides a JSON REST API covering core entities and actions including items, apps, spaces/workspaces, organizations, tasks, files, comments, users, and more through documented endpoints and conventions.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Podio MCP server or MCP documentation was found on Podio's developer site.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official limits page documents 1,000 API calls per hour generally, 250 calls per hour for resource-intensive calls, X-Rate-Limit headers, and HTTP 420 responses when exceeded.
raw_result.notable_signals: Public developer docs are detailed and current enough to support first-pass toolkit development; pricing explicitly includes API access on the Free tier and add-ons for more volume.

result.description: No-code work management platform for building customizable team workspaces, workflows, and project processes.
result.auth_notes: Podio documents OAuth 2.0 authentication plus an app authentication flow; the auth page lists server-side, client-side, username/password, and app authentication flows using client ID and client secret.
